selection_basis: The excerpt or its immediate historical occasion provides a substantive stimulus for the question.
The keyed development clearly predates and directly shapes the source's position, policy, or occasion.
Biographical trivia, generic inaugural ceremony, weak thematic association, and historically disputed causal claims are excluded.

items.stem: The bitterness that surrounded Adams as he delivered this address stemmed most directly from which of the following?
The economic distress that Bryan invokes in defense of his 'cause of humanity' was contributed to most directly by which event?
The petitions that Calhoun refuses to receive in the excerpt were most directly a product of which of the following developments?
The public disillusionment that led the president to deliver the address in the excerpt was most directly caused by which of the following foreign and energy-related developments of the 1970s?
The tone of humility and moral appeal in Carter's inaugural excerpt contributed most directly as a response to which of the following developments?
The presence of Chinese laborers referenced in the excerpt was most directly a result of which earlier development?
The optimistic 'readjustment to the results of the great conflict' Coolidge describes was made possible in part by which of the following political developments?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the concerns about 'national security' expressed by Roosevelt in this 1940 address?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the sense of threat 'from without' that Roosevelt describes in the excerpt?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the mood of 'regret' and 'new hope' the author expresses upon surveying the world 'after the great storm'?
The circumstances surrounding Hayes's assumption of the presidency, referenced in the excerpt as a transition of office, were most directly the result of which of the following?
The extension of the land benefit in the excerpt to immigrants who 'filed a declaration of intention to become' citizens was most directly encouraged by which prior development?
Jackson's pledge to 'preserve their liberty' by administering the Government responsibly was shaped most directly by which crisis unfolding as he took his second oath?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the peaceful transfer of power that occasioned the address in the excerpt?
The reference in the excerpt to 'the war with a powerful nation' was most directly a result of which of the following?
The central campaign debate that most directly propelled McKinley to the presidency referenced in the excerpt concerned which of the following issues?
The constitutional crisis that preceded the address in the excerpt was most directly precipitated by which of the following?
Which of the following most directly contributed to the escalated American involvement in Vietnam that Nixon inherited by 1969?
The framework for governing and eventually admitting new territory reflected in the excerpt was most directly shaped by which prior concern of the founding generation?
The 'period of supreme test' that Roosevelt describes in the excerpt was most directly caused by which of the following?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the conditions of 'stagnation and despair' that the author references in the excerpt?
The concern about 'the real peril of inaction' expressed in the excerpt was most directly a response to which of the following developments?
The sectional tensions confronting the administration inaugurated in the excerpt were contributed to most directly by which of the following?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the enlarged 'business of the government' that the author describes as necessitating new buildings?
Sojourner Truth's own ability to speak publicly against slavery was made possible most directly by which of the following?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the ideas about religion and government expressed in the excerpt?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the accommodationist strategy that Booker T. Washington articulates in the excerpt?
The urgency to organize a national government, reflected in Washington's assuming office in the excerpt, was intensified most directly by which crisis of the 1780s?
Which of the following contributed most directly to the Democratic capture of the presidency that Wilson describes in the excerpt?
